channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Most immigrants at risk of deportation from US are Christian, report finds
item.description: As many as four in five immigrants at risk of deportation from the United States are Christian, according to a new report that calls on their fellow believers to consider the impact of aggressive deportation policies pursued under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smith, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court is at stake in a race that drew powerful political interests
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Majority control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court will be decided Tuesday in a race that broke records for spending and has become a proxy battle for the nation’s political fights, pitting a candidate backed by President Donald Trump against a Democratic-aligned challenger.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An ‘administrative error’ sent a Maryland man to an El Salvador prison, ICE says
item.description: President Donald Trump’ s administration has acknowledged mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison last month, but is arguing against returning him to the United States because of his alleged gang ties and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Last US soldier found dead after Lithuania training accident. 3 others identified
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The final U.S. soldier who went missing in Lithuania has been found dead, bringing to an end a massive weeklong search for the four service members whose armored vehicle was pulled from a swampy training area, the U.S. military said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge blocks firing of intelligence agency employees who worked on DEI programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge in Virginia blocked the Trump administration's move to fire more than a dozen intelligence agency employees who worked on diversity, equity and inclusion programs. U.S. District Judge Anthony J.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Northern Michigan
item.description: The tip of Michigan's mitten struggled through another day without electricity Tuesday as restless residents tried to stay warm while utilities scrambled to restore power in a region waylaid by weekend freezing rain that brought down countless trees and poles.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: San Diego migrant shelter closes doors with numbers plummeting after Trump immigration crackdown: report
item.description: A San Diego migrant shelter is reportedly closing its doors and reverting to its original purpose after the number of asylum seekers dramatically drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 people are injured after a truck crashed into a building and pedestrians in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A box truck crashed into a building Tuesday afternoon in Boston’s Chinatown neighborhood and hit several pedestrians before flipping onto its side, city officials said. The driver and five others were injured in what authorities said appears to have been an accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security Administration probes origin of website portal outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration said Tuesday that it is investigating the root cause of website outages that have affected the “my Social Security” portal where recipients access their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire kills New York cat sanctuary founder and dozens of animals he rescued
item.description: A fire burned down a Long Island cat shelter, killing its founder, who lived there, and at least 59 of the felines he rescued, authorities said. The fire at the Happy Cat Sanctuary in the hamlet of Medford was reported shortly after 7 a.m. Monday. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi said Tuesday that she has directed prosecutors to seek the death penalty against Luigi Mangione in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, following through on the president's campaign promise to vigorously pursue capital punishment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Potential albino pygmy killer whale spotted off Hawaii
item.description: "We noticed there was a white baby with them," said Travis Woo, a safety diver. "My initial reaction was euphoria.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read's retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Jury selection began Tuesday in the retrial of Karen Read, less than a year after a judge declared a mistrial on charges that she was responsible for the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dave Coulier shares big news about family, cancer fight
item.description: Dave Coulier, who shared he had been diagnosed with Non-Hodgkin lymphoma last year, had some exciting news to share on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, WFLA, NBC

item.title: High school seniors reject Columbia University as Trump admin reviews money for Ivy League schools
item.description: A college admissions consultant reportedly said Columbia University's brand has been "tarnished" while the school devolves into chaos stemming from anti-Israel riots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Israel Ivy League student who sued Trump dismisses case, says he has voluntarily left US
item.description: Momodou Taal, a Cornell University foreign grad student who had his visa revoked after protesting against Israel, is dismissing his lawsuit against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Investigation into death of former Yankees player's son turns to carbon monoxide: reports
item.description: Investigators in Costa Rica say carbon monoxide may instead be to blame for the death of Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Intel Democrats call for damage assessment on Signal chat
item.description: Democrats on the House Intelligence Committee on Monday requested an independent assessment into Trump administration officials' use of Signal to discuss an airstrike, asking that Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard initiate such a process and delegate it to another official.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Cast for Beatles biopics revealed: Paul Mescal, Barry Keoghan, Joseph Quinn and Harris Dickinson to star
item.description: Sony Pictures kicked off the annual CinemaCon convention and trade show in Las Vegas on Monday by touting their four upcoming biopics told from the perspective of each member of The Beatles.
item.dc:creator: Krysta Fauria, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey state trooper accused of fatally shooting his dog, assaulting local police officer
item.description: A New Jersey state trooper allegedly shot and killed his dog, vandalized a stop sign and assaulted a local police officer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colleges in ICE's deportation crosshairs shelled out discounts, financial aid to illegal immigrants: GOP rep
item.description: Some colleges and universities being targeted by ICE under the Trump administration have boasted that they give large financial aid packages to illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 20 staffers accused of abusing students or not reporting it at Pennsylvania charter school
item.description: Twenty staffers at a suburban Philadelphia charter school are facing charges related to the alleged physical abuse of students using painful, unapproved techniques to restrain and punish youths in a program meant to help them deal with emotional issues, authorities announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains found in California decades ago ID'd as missing New York 13-year-old
item.description: A 13-year-old girl who was found dead along a river in California's Santa Cruz County three decades ago was recently identified through DNA testing, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: You need to make nearly $117K to afford a typical US home: Study
item.description: The household income needed to afford a typical U.S. home has jumped nearly 50% since 2020, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: A lawsuit by inmates is challenging Tennessee's plans to resume executions. Here's how
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Nine Tennessee death row inmates are suing the state over its push for a new round of lethal injections after an execution was abruptly called off in 2022 and a follow-up investigation found scores of missteps in several executions.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre says she has four days to live
item.description: Giuffre didn’t disclose when the accident happened, but said it has been the “worst start to the new year.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 US soldiers found dead in armored vehicle in Lithuania; 1 still missing
item.description: Three of the U.S. Army soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have been found dead in their armored vehicle that was pulled from a swampy area early Monday, according to U.S. Army Europe and Africa Command. Another soldier is still missing.
item.dc:creator: By LIUDAS DAPKUS and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 3 killed, 3 hurt from same family when tree falls on car during storm
item.description: Three people were killed after a tree struck their vehicle in Michigan as strong storms moved through the area Sunday, deputies say.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, Fox 5

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Judge weighs request to withhold investigation records in deaths of Gene Hackman and wife
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A New Mexico court is weighing whether to block the disclosure of an array of records from an investigation into the deaths of actor Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, at the request of the couple’s estate.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tsunami warning lifted after 7.0 earthquake near Tonga in the South Pacific
item.description: A strong 7.0 magnitude earthquake hit near Tonga, prompting an initial tsunami warning that was later lifted for the Pacific island country
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, Fox 5
